patch.value: Reduce ESM module-resolution startup overhead (~85ms) via core bundling

CPU profile of pm list shows ~85ms in Node ESM resolution/compilation (compileSourceTextModule, getPackageScopeConfig, realpathSync, defaultResolve, deserializePackageJSON) because dist is unbundled tsc output (~170 files). pm-gt82 explored esbuild bundling but it is NOT the shipped build. Evaluate shipping a bundled core entry (extensions still dynamically imported) to cut per-command + startup cost across the board. Investigate why pm-gt82 bundle did not ship.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Evidence 2026-05-27: CPU profiles of 'pm get' (249ms) and 'pm list' (345ms) show the per-command cost is ~entirely Node ESM module resolution/compilation (compileSourceTextModule, getPackageScopeConfig, realpathSync, defaultResolve, deserializePackageJSON ~85-90ms) over ~170 unbundled tsc files — there is NO item-specific hot-path cost left in get. pm-gt82 mis-benchmarked the bundle on 'pm --version', which short-circuits in cli.ts BEFORE main.js loads, so the bundle looked slower; real commands (list/get/search/--help) would benefit. Correct approach: esbuild --bundle --splitting --format=esm --platform=node --packages=external (keep node_modules + dynamic extension import() external; --splitting preserves the 4 lazy register-* modules and per-command laziness). esbuild is NOT currently a devDependency. Compile cache (enableCompileCache) is already effective (~1.24x) and does NOT remove resolution cost. This is the single biggest remaining startup lever (~90ms on every command, ~185ms->~110ms startup). Deferred to its own focused PR with extension/MCP/version/sentry-sourcemap verification — too risky to bundle inside the perf+reliability PR.

value.text: Evidence 2026-05-29: shipped idempotent esbuild core bundling in the build pipeline. Focused gates passed: pnpm build, pnpm typecheck, node scripts/bundle-cli.mjs repeated successfully, and 9-file regression suite passed (332 tests). Current bundled command timings over 5 runs: --version avg 35ms; get pm-th6y --depth brief avg 225ms; list-open --limit 5 avg 302ms; search performance --limit 5 avg 299ms; health summary avg 303ms; contracts flags avg 296ms.
